program: Travel English -- Europe Without a Translator
hub_subtitle: Travel English for the airport, the check-in counter, the hotel and passport control — so that Europe needs no translator

lesson.menu_desc: Saying the plan with going to, buying the ticket at the machine, and stopping a stranger to ask the way -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 5 -- Getting Around the City
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rosângela | Lesson 5 -- Getting Around the City

listenings.text: Good morning, and welcome to the Lisbon metro. Please listen to this information before you travel. First, your ticket. The ticket machines are in front of you, next to the entrance. You can choose English on the screen: press the small flag, on the top right. On the machine you can buy a one-way ticket, for one trip only, or a round trip, to go and to come back on the same day. The fare for one trip is one euro and eighty. A round trip is three euros and sixty. It is the same price, but you only stand in the line one time. Second, the platform. After the ticket machine, look at the big sign above your head. The sign does not give you the name of your station: it gives you the name of the last station on the line. So look for the last station, and walk to that platform. Third, the transfer. Some trips are direct, and some are not. If you need to transfer, you change from one line to another inside the station, and you do not pay again: your ticket is good for the whole trip. The station for a transfer is Baixa-Chiado, in the center. And last, the tram and the bus. Your metro ticket does not work on the tram. For the tram and for the bus, you buy a different ticket, at the bus stop or from the driver. Thank you very much, and have a good day in Lisbon.

Hello. My name is Beatriz, and I am waiting for the tram, like you. Is this your first day in Lisbon? Oh, then let me tell you what I am going to do this week. You can copy me! Tomorrow I am going to take tram twenty-eight, in the morning, early. Why early? Because at ten o'clock it is full of people, and you are going to travel standing up, with your nose in somebody's back. Not nice. Tram twenty-eight goes up the hill to Alfama, and it is the most beautiful trip in this city. On Wednesday I am going to take the train to Sintra, and I am going to see the palace. And on Thursday? Nothing. On Thursday I am going to sit in a café and do absolutely nothing. That is my favorite plan of the week. Now, one small piece of advice. I would rather take the metro than a taxi, always. The traffic here is terrible, and the metro is fast and cheap. But at night, after eleven, the metro is closed, so I would rather take a ride-share. And my husband? Oh, he does not like the tram. He says it is too slow, so he is going to walk everywhere. He is going to arrive tired, and I am going to arrive happy. Well, that is my tram. Enjoy the city!

items.situation: You are leaving the hotel and you want to go to the city center. Ask at the front desk, and say what you are going to visit.
tips: Could you tell me how to get to the city center, please?
We are going to visit the old cathedral this morning.

items.situation: You are in front of the ticket machine. You are going to Belém, and you are going to come back after lunch. Say what you buy, and why.
tips: I am going to buy a round trip, not a one-way ticket.
We are going to come back this afternoon, so a round trip is better.

items.situation: You are standing on the platform with your husband, and you are not sure it is the right one. Ask.
tips: Excuse me, is this the right platform for Belém?
Is this the right platform for the airport?

items.situation: You want to know if the trip is long, because your husband is tired. Ask.
tips: How long does it take to get to Sintra?
How long does it take to get to the airport, please?

items.situation: The agent says you have to change lines in the middle of the trip. Say it back to him, to check you understood.
tips: So I am going to transfer at Baixa-Chiado. Is that right?
I am going to change lines. Which line am I going to take?

items.situation: Your husband wants a taxi. The traffic is terrible. Say what you prefer, and why.
tips: I would rather take the metro than a taxi. It is faster.
The traffic is terrible. I would rather take the metro.

items.situation: It is eleven at night, you are tired, and the metro is closed. Say your plan.
tips: We are going to take a ride-share back to the hotel.
I would rather take a ride-share than walk at night.

items.situation: Somebody at the hotel asks: "What are you going to do tomorrow?" Answer with two plans.
tips: Tomorrow we are going to take the tram to Alfama, early.
In the afternoon I am going to visit the tower, and we are going to walk by the river.
